Watching The Lion King at the Lyceum Theatre is an experience that begins the moment you walk through the doors. The theatre itself is absolutely beautiful, with a classic and elegant architecture that immediately sets the tone for the night. The seats are very comfortable, the layout is well designed, and the overall atmosphere is fantastic, full of excitement and anticipation.

The show is truly extraordinary. From the very first scene, you are completely drawn in by the power of the music, the creativity of the staging, and the incredible energy of the production. Every single actor delivers an outstanding performance, combining strong vocals, expressive movement, and genuine emotion. The music is powerful and immersive, creating moments that give you goosebumps and stay with you long after the curtain falls.

The production quality is exceptional. The costumes, choreography, and visual effects are all executed with incredible attention to detail, making the story come alive in a magical and unforgettable way. Everything flows seamlessly, keeping you engaged from beginning to end and making time fly by.

This is definitely something you should not miss if you are visiting London. More than just a musical, it is a complete and memorable experience that captures the heart and reminds you why live theatre is so special. An absolute must see.

Lyceum Theatre ‚Äď London

A very beautiful and classic theatre with so much character. The interior is stunning and really gives that old-school London theatre charm.

The only downside for me was that it felt a bit warm inside ‚ÄĒ would be even better with stronger air-conditioning, especially when the theatre is full.

That aside, the experience was amazing. This was my first time watching The Lion King, and it absolutely blew me away. The costumes, stage design, live music, and performers were incredible ‚ÄĒ you can really see the full effort and passion put into the show.

A truly memorable experience and definitely worth watching at least once in your life.

A really beautiful minor league ballpark. Kind of a throwback: no cursing, no booing, no disorderly conduct. Just a great crowd in a great ballpark having a terrific time. Probably seats around 6000 people. Clear sight lines no matter where you sit.  Plenty of parking. We had a really good time. ‚ôŅÔłŹ

We always have a great time at the Rumble Ponies games‚ÄĒit‚Äôs truly the highlight of our summer! The stadium is clean and well maintained, with several food and drink options. I especially love the liquor/wine slushy stand‚ÄĒso good! They do a great job with themed nights and giveaways, and Dog Day is always a favorite! Parking is easy, cheap, and super convenient. The staff has always been pleasant and helpful too. My only wish is to see the mascots out more often, but I get that those costumes get hot! We‚Äôre already looking forward to our next game!!

Mirabito Stadium is a nice place to see a baseball game! Sightlines are excellent from nearly every seat in the park. You are never too far away from the action. During warmups, it is easy to see and even talk to the players. The stadium does offer nice views of the surrounding hills and you can also see some buildings in downtown Binghamton. The historic Marconi Tower is right next to the stadium and is also visible from the stadium.
The stadium offers excellent food choices. Lupo‚Äôs Grill offers spiedies; a regional classic. Off the right field line, there is a nice deck you can watch the game from while having food or drink. New this year is a large video scoreboard in right field. There are also some picnic tables behind the right field wall. Food prices are typically high, but the prices are comparable to other minor league ballparks. The Rumble Ponies have a team store near the front of the stadium that offers an extensive selection of Rumble Ponies merchandise.
Ticket prices are very reasonable and you can get good seats for a low price. The team provided plenty of entertainment and prize activities during breaks in the action. It is best to park in a paid parking lot during the game. Parking spots are available near the stadium, but there are a lot of foul balls that leave the stadium and hit cars that are parked near the stadium.
All and all you can‚Äôt go wrong at Mirabito Stadium. It offers good seating at fair prices with a chance to see the MLB players of tomorrow.

Stopped for a quick breakfast during my last trip to NYC. The staff were so nice and helpful. Ordered these very indulgent pancakes that were tasty. They had lots of fresh ingredients. The prices had increased compared to other reviews photos I had seen. The prices were still very reasonable. Would definitely visit again.

I was looking for breakfast place not far from our hotel in Manhattan.  I saw in Maps there was a deli at the corner of the block we were staying in.  So the next morning we walked down to the deli at the corner of West 54th and 8th Avenue.  Walked right up to the counter and saw examples of what we wanted (like the plastic models of food we see in Japan).  We ordered two small Mochas which were freshly steamed and a few minutes later we were enjoying our breakfast.

It‚Äôs a small space but ample for a quick meal - and the food was very good.  I had French Toast with bacon - the toast was good and thick, battered just right and sprinkled with powdered sugar - the bacon was not skimpy but appropriately thick and more of a center cut with a good amount of meat (rather than fat and gristle).  My wife had two eggs over easy served on avocado toast with lettuce and tomato.  A hearty meal to start a day of sightseeing.

We came back another day later in the week and shared a croissant breakfast sandwich - the croissant was very fresh and the combo was tasty.

Since I arrived in NYC, I was searching for a main place to get a good experience of a American breakfast. This place just stole my food heart! Yesterday I took pancakes with bananas and strawberries. It was so good that I had to eat here again this morning. I recommend this place to everyone who wants to not overpay for food and wants to enjoy an true American breakfast! (and yes, the food you‚Äôre seeing on the second picture was all for me ūüėĚ)

Had breakfast with my work friends and we celebrated one of our friends birthday. Food is divine. Their burritos and eggs benedict,  you would want more. Service was fantastic too and a very cozy ambiance. Perfect for family and friends. We asked for a dessert that we can use for blowing of candles but they dont have,  instead they offered lemon ricotta pancakes,  it was good too and they have it on the house. so nice of them. Highly recommend and will definitely come back.

Second, the food was 10/10 both times, I got the New York bagel, and the breakfast burrito and both were delicious and huge portions. Lastly, the service was great. I had Jennifer as a server and she was very kind and attentive to me which I always appreciate when sitting down to eat. Check it out next time you‚Äôre in the area!

It‚Äôs always full of energy, busy, crowded and maybe not the prettiest station in the world but it‚Äôs highly functional for travelers who can use Subway, AmTrak and LIRR services from here to get where they need to go. The food court has a variety of options, from Shake Shack to Raising Cane‚Äôs, from Chopt to Playa Bowls and there‚Äôs a Walgreens that can pretty much help every needs one may have while waiting to commute or travel around New York! I‚Äôm personally here every day and I must say that Pennsylvania Station is safe, with a lot of MTA Police constantly around and clean for the high amount of traffic that passes through daily. It‚Äôs I place in town that I love! Don‚Äôt expect luxury, especially during rush hours, but Penn Station does the job!

Out of all the museums I‚Äôve visited so far, this one feels the most comfortable to me. Maybe I‚Äôm just a fan of na√Įve and so-called ‚Äúdegenerate‚ÄĚ art ‚ÄĒ but there‚Äôs something about this place that feels intimate rather than overwhelming.

You‚Äôre not flooded with an impossible volume of works to rush through. The curation is thoughtful ‚ÄĒ the placement, the sequence, the dialogue between paintings and decorative objects all feel intentional and balanced. You can actually breathe and take it in.

I was especially in awe of the architectural design itself. The building ‚ÄĒ a former Gilded Age mansion on Museum Mile ‚ÄĒ adds another layer to the experience. It feels refined yet warm.

The collection of German and Austrian art and design is exceptional, featuring works by artists like Gustav Klimt (including Portrait of Adele Bloch-Bauer I), Egon Schiele, and Wassily Kandinsky, alongside pieces from the Wiener Werkst√§tte and Bauhaus movements. It‚Äôs a powerful combination of fine art and design.

I also loved that you can dine inside. If the caf√© on the first floor is full, there‚Äôs another option downstairs ‚ÄĒ equally charming.

Just note: no photography in the galleries. And honestly, that‚Äôs part of the beauty. You‚Äôre forced to slow down, look closely, and let the works speak to you.

Loved every part of it.

Beautiful museum of German and Austrian art on Museum Mile of 5th Ave. The building itself is historic and a work of art, and the collection is stunning. Lovely gift shops, a cafe, and friendly staff make this a very worth while stop on your art sight seeing tour. I went for a guided tour and learned a lot, saw provocative and thought provoking pieces and was impressed at the variety in each exhibit. 10/10 highly recommend.

Located on the upper east side right across from Central Park, I had no expectations other than reviewers complaining about staff harassing them.  You enter via an unimpressive side entrance and immediately must go through a security check - the staff were friendly but stern - no liquids allowed to enter at all (we were forced to drink our bottled water on the sidewalk).  Once inside you purchase tickets from a very friendly staff person ($28, similar to the $30 at MET, MOMA, and Frick) and are directed to complimentary coat check in the basement.  You are may WEAR your coat / jacket/ sweatshirt but the moment you take any of these off, their security / staff will immediately tell you to put them back on or take them to coat check.  FYI - we found the building to be very warm in the winter.    No photographs allowed anywhere and the minute you take out your phone, they immediately have eyes on what you are doing.   That said, they do have some nice audio commentary for many of their pieces so it is worthwhile to download the app (FREE) and bring your earbuds.  Their rules are published so I suppose fair game for them to so vigilantly enforce them, but it is the most strict of any museum I have been to and did make the whole visit a little less relaxing (if not for myself, than for other people always being reprimanded). Do yourself a favor and just plan to follow the rules.  While the museum in an older home, it definitely has more a museum vibe than a luxurious mansion vibe. Overall we had a nice 30-40 minute visit - this isn't a large collection.  Yes they have some amazing Klimts, as well as some other nice pieces......but if you aren't a big Klimt fan, I am not sure I would go out of my way or pay  this relatively higher price (considering the smaller collection) compared to other much more varied and larger volume at the other museums.   I am glad I went and had a nice visit (big Klimt fan) but can't see myself making much effort to return very often.

This was my very first Broadway show, so this review is from a total novice but I absolutely loved it.

We arrived about 10 minutes late due to a train delay, but the show hadn‚Äôt started and the staff was incredibly kind and efficient in getting us to our seats. The theater was packed, and we were the last two to be seated. We sat in the middle mezzanine, and thanks to the sloped layout, our view was completely unobstructed. Honestly, there isn‚Äôt a bad seat in the house.

The sound and lighting were excellent, and the scene transitions were seamless, everything felt effortless and magical. Restrooms are available on both the upstairs and lower levels; skip the upstairs lines and head downstairs.

Overall, a fantastic experience and perfect for solo, date, family, or group outings. I can‚Äôt wait to return!

Death Becomes Her is pure Broadway joy, funny, flashy, and wildly entertaining from the first moment to the final bow.

The show is laugh-out-loud hilarious, packed with sharp comedy and deliciously over the top moments that know exactly what they‚Äôre doing. The leading ladies are absolutely incredible. Jennifer Simard and Betsy Wolfe are a dream pairing, fearless, funny, vocally thrilling, and completely committed. Their chemistry is electric, and they bring the perfect mix of camp, heart, and star power. And Michelle Williams is spectacular. She commands the stage with confidence, warmth, and powerhouse presence, adding another layer of excitement to an already phenomenal cast.

The costumes are stunning, bold, glamorous, and jaw-dropping. Every look feels like a showstopper. The lighting is absolutely on point, elevating the musical and enhancing the magic with precision and style.

Death Becomes Her is Broadway at its most fun, big laughs, incredible performances, and a visual feast. If you want a night of pure entertainment with performers at the top of their game, this show is a must-see.

I want to address an issue regarding this business. The food is good, but unfortunately the customer service is very disappointing. The girl working at the counter is rude and unprofessional, and her behavior is unacceptable. She often gives off a judgmental attitude, as if customers are a burden to her. It honestly seems like she does not enjoy working there.

The first time this happened, I came in during my break since I work in the building next door. I come here quite often, almost every day. That day I wasn‚Äôt feeling well and just wanted to sit on the second floor for a few minutes. After about 10 minutes, she approached me and told me I had to buy something or leave, and even said she would call the police if I didn‚Äôt. I understand that businesses may have policies, and I respect that. However, there is a professional and respectful way to communicate those policies, especially in the hospitality industry.

The second incident happened not long ago when I politely asked if there was a charger available. She completely ignored me and treated me as if I were asking for something unreasonable. The way she handled the situation made me feel uncomfortable and disrespected.

I believe management should pay closer attention to customer service standards. Good food alone is not enough to maintain a successful business if customers feel unwelcome.

History of Phap Van Parish
A Brief History

The Phap Van parish has existed for a long time. In the 1927 census of churches in the Western Tonkin Diocese, the Phap Van church was described as being 22 meters long and 9.5 meters wide.

In 2014, due to the lack of a church, Cardinal Peter Nguyen Van Nhon allowed the parish to celebrate Mass at the private residences of Mr. Dung and Mr. Huy. With a large population, spiritual needs were an indispensable part of their lives. In 2015, under the care of Father Joseph Nguyen Ngoc Hinh of Thinh Liet (Ke Set) parish, the Hanoi City People's Committee returned a corner of a pond to the parish, where the current Phap Van church is located. The parish made efforts to build a temporary shelter for celebrating Mass. From then on, all religious activities were celebrated. Since 2015, all Masses, including Holy Week Masses, have been celebrated at the parish. Despite the difficulties and cramped facilities, the parishioners' enthusiasm has been met and continues to grow.

As an area with a large migrant population, the number of people attending the liturgy has steadily increased. Especially since Archbishop Joseph Vu Van Thien visited and celebrated Mass at the end of 2018, the religious life in the parish has flourished. In 2020, the parish was preparing the necessary documents to request the Archbishop's approval for its elevation to parish status. On November 21, 2020, Archbishop Joseph signed the decision to establish the Phap Van parish, appointing Father Joseph Nguyen Ngoc Hinh as the parish priest of Yen Duyen and Phap Van.

reviews.text:
787 Coffee is my absolute favorite coffee spot in NYC. The flavors are unmatched ‚ÄĒ Dulce de Leche Latte? Coquito Latte? Say less. They always have the most unique coffee blends, and every cup supports farmers and families.

The decor is cute, cozy, and low-key IG worthy: loveseats, velvet chairs, even swings.

I used to frequent the Harlem location on 129th and was so hype to find one in Brooklyn. When I walked in (clearly excited), Nettie greeted me with the warmest welcome. She answered all my questions, gave the best recommendation, and still kept everything moving behind the counter. The kindest and sweetest barista, such a gem.

As a native New Yorker ‚ÄĒ a Black American who grew up around Puerto Rican and Nuyorican friends and neighbors ‚ÄĒ the celebration of Puerto Rican culture here feels familiar. Not trendy. Familiar. Neuvayolllll.

I‚Äôll definitely be back. And Nettie better be there too!

We came here for the weekend celebrating my fianc√©s birthday. I came just to try it out and loved it, I ended up coming back the next morning before our commute back home to get another coffee. Friendly staff both days, very relaxed vibe. I enjoyed it !

We found this spot by accident and ended up loving it. The coffee had great flavor, and the employee who took our order shared that they‚Äôre third-generation coffee roasters. Definitely worth the stop.

Beautiful stadium on the grounds of the US tennis center.
This is a great stadium with great seats, good concessions and great staff.
I've been here for AEW wrestling and not tennis but it's a great venue for other things than tennis as well.

Very conveniently accessible by the subway. The US Open is really well organized with a lot of freebies, experiences, lounge access & great food/drink options! All the stadiums are pretty close to each other. The Arthur Ashe is the biggest one and usually the best players play there! A great experience watching the beat tennis players in action

Arthur Ashe Stadium is the best. It's the biggest tennis stadium in the world and it feels huge, but you can still see everything. The best part is the roof. You never have to worry about rain and can watch the whole match. The atmosphere is crazy, especially during night matches. If you're going to the US Open, you have to get a ticket for Arthur Ashe.

reviews.text: We have our Christmas Day prayer there after walking 2.4km from the hotel we stayed. The choir was amazing. So beautiful voice and the ochestra was amazingly nice. The church is beautiful and unique in colour. It's free. No need ticket

Nice looking Catholic Church. The service I attended was in Vietnamese, and it was very  well organised, and all attendees seemed very engaged. However, I was stopped by a group of older women who told me that if I wasn't a Catholic, I shouldn't be there. This made me feel very disappointed about the Catholic community in Hanoi. I have been to many holy Christian sites, including the Vatican city and Jerusalem. Not one time I was questioned and asked to leave for not being Christian. It is my understanding that all are welcome in the house of the Lord. I told them they shouldn't stop anyone from entering the Church. Unfortunately, older people in Hanoi have a lot of power, so they do not listen to someone younger than them. Having said that, the younger people seemed very friendly. The toilets are clean and free to use.

A good place to camp for the weekend. Large space, many trees,
Enjoy playing away from the hustle and bustle of Hanoi cityūü•įūü•į

reviews.text: It was incredible. It was clean and beautiful to sit. The show was more than I could ask. It was amazing incredible I'd go again for sure

The Imperial Theatre in uptown Saint John is nothing short of a gem, and our recent experience attending "The Nutcracker" ballet truly showcased its charm and elegance. From the moment we stepped inside, we were enveloped in an atmosphere of historic grandeur and artistic vibrancy.

The theatre itself is beautifully styled, with its stunning architecture and intricate details that transport you to a different era. The plush seating and intimate layout ensure that every patron feels connected to the performance, making it a perfect venue for a ballet as beloved as "The Nutcracker." The soft glow of the chandeliers set the stage for an enchanting evening, and the acoustics are superb, allowing every note of Tchaikovsky's timeless score to resonate through the hall.

As for the performance, it was a magical experience! The dancers brought the story to life with grace and precision, captivating the audience with their skill and artistry. Each scene was a feast for the eyes, with vibrant costumes and imaginative set designs that truly captured the spirit of the holiday season. The blend of classical ballet with the festive atmosphere made for an unforgettable night.

The staff at the Imperial Theatre were warm and welcoming, enhancing our experience with their attentiveness and enthusiasm. Whether you‚Äôre a local or just visiting, catching a performance here is a must. The Imperial Theatre is not just a venue; it's a celebration of culture and the arts in Saint John. We left the theatre feeling uplifted and inspired, already looking forward to our next visit! ūüĆü
We visited St John and decided to check out the theatre.  The tour guides were very knowledgeable and very friendly.  This place is a well maintained treasure.  Amazing place.

Located on the lower floor of this unique multipurpose building, sits Cafe Bewilderment. One of the charms of the cafe is the decor, which is super clean and plants hanging from the ceiling. So you get a comfortable cozy space that‚Äôs perfect for socializing, and listening to live music playing in the background. The bar area is perfect if you want to enjoy one of their well made cocktails or non alcoholic drinks. The Cafe Bewilderment menu is on the small side, even smaller as the night goes on. But you can find small bites including sandwiches on there. There‚Äôs even a selection of bake goods that is well worth trying out.

Grilled cheese- loaded with the right amount of bacon jam. Had a really good salty sweet flavor profile.

Clover & sage cocktail- amazing drink

Butter tart- tasted just like pecan pie.. I could eat a dozen of these

I stopped by Caf√© Bewilderment today for the first time and it instantly became my new go-to spot. The atmosphere is beautiful and cozy, and really welcoming.
I sat at the bar and asked the woman working there if it was okay to sit even if I wasn‚Äôt ordering alcohol, and she was so kind. She said, ‚ÄúOf course you can sit here! You don‚Äôt have to order food or coffee just stay as long as you like.‚ÄĚ That immediately made me feel comfortable.

I ordered a cardamom latte and a warmed lemon blueberry scone (around $14), and both were incredible. The coffee was smooth, the flavors were perfect and rich, and the scone was delicious. I stayed for about two and a half hours to finish some work and loved every minute.

The staff were genuinely friendly and the vibes were amazing. I visit caf√©s often in both Toronto and Buffalo, and this one really stands out, the energy here just makes you want to slow down and stay awhile. Can‚Äôt wait to come back and try their brunch menu!
I had been wanting to check out this cafe for some time now, and it did not disappoint! Beautiful space, tasty coffee and food. Looking forward to going back one evening for cocktails!

My first time here at Fresco Cafe, and I‚Äôm extremely impressed! The Mozzarella Sticks was good, but the Hallumi Salad was out of this world, so fresh so good, I will have to come back! The mushroom Truffle Tostini is amazing, together with the sauce it is even better! The cinnamon Latte goes well together and I loved it! their pastries looks amazing! I got a chocolate halva Haman Tash it was delicious! I will be back!

Had breakfast this morning by fresco.
I‚Äôm really happy they opened. The food and vibe is awesome! We had shakshuka and American breakfast.
Food was delicious and fresh. The shakshuka was honestly one of the best ive ever had!
We were lucky and got a chocolate croissant out of the oven. It was insane. They should put up a Krispy cr√®me style light to let people know when then they have it fresh pastries!

Fresco in Pomona, NY is a fantastic spot with an elevated menu and a cozy, modern vibe. I ordered the Mushroom Truffle Tostini and it was absolutely delicious, rich, savory, and perfectly balanced with that deep truffle flavor. What really impressed me was that it even came with a little side salad, which added a nice fresh touch to the dish. Everything was plated beautifully and tasted just as good as it looked. The service was friendly and attentive, and you can tell the kitchen takes real pride in what they serve. If you‚Äôre in Rockland County and looking for a classy yet approachable dining experience, Fresco is a great choice.

Had a great time visiting the zoo. Though it was a chilly day so few parts of the zoo were empty like the poultry area zebras, hyenas etc but we had a good luck to see these amazing and majestic animals. Polar bear and snow leopard were very comfortable due to the chilled weather and it was a treat to see them play. Recommended for children to must visit with their parents and have a fun outing.

Wonderful, small zoo with great exhibits. Some exhibits were under construction and there weren‚Äôt any animals in them. Other than that it is a great morning or afternoon to spend with nature and its animals. There is a lovely walkway around a park that is disability accessible outside the zoo as well.

Went on free Thursday in October was great weather not hot or cold was plenty of people for a weekday.  Got to see the baby red panda were so cute .We had a burger at the burger restaurant was very good weird the pop was more than the burger! Spent couple of hours there got to see everything loved the polar bear and giraffes and the rainforest.

LB (Elbi), meaning _my heart_ in Arabic, is more than just coffee.

A story of roots, resilience, and community in New York City.

![Image 1](https://images.squarespace-cdn.com/content/v1/68a4819d230af445c7e6a618/0bf4f0e7-3b9a-47fb-baf9-f717bedcb8b7/23_L_B_Coffee_January_2026_HR_Credit_Marc_J_Franklin.jpg)

When we found our little corner on East 84th Street we decided to make a place to gather, share stories, and bring a piece of mediterranean warmth and hospitality to the Upper East Side.

We‚Äôre rooted in tradition, poured with love, and have designed L B COFFEE to bring people together. Every detail, from the olive tree in our design to the Mediterranean bites, honors our roots while embracing New York‚Äôs fast rhythm.

Our menu is simple: artisan coffee paired with Mediterranean-inspired bites.

We believe small businesses change neighborhoods. We hope to grow into a space that creates experiences and elevated moments through events, community gatherings, and our products.

At the core of it all: community, heart, and delicious flavors.

We look forward to welcoming you to our shop,

L B COFFEE FAMILY

## About This Theatre

Opened in 1910, the Lunt-Fontanne Theatre was originally named The Globe, after Shakespeare‚Äôs theatre in England. It had a retractable roof to enable the theatre to stay cool and open during the summer. For nearly two decades, the theatre housed a variety of plays and musicals, until 1932 when it was turned into a movie house.

In 1958, the theatre was gutted and rebuilt in its present configuration as a legitimate theatre. It was renamed in honor of America‚Äôs foremost husband-wife acting couple, Alfred Lunt and Lynn Fontanne, who starred in its first production, _The Visit_. Theatregoers can enjoy a selection of photographs from their private collection on display throughout the lobby areas.

_Death Becomes Her_ is now playing at the Lunt-Fontanne Theatre.

The Lunt-Fontanne has 1,505 seats and is one of [The Nederlander Organization](http://nederlander.com/)‚Äės nine Broadway theatres.

## Welcome to 

cafe bewilderment

##### We invite you to be wild and be: 

curious, connected, and completely yourself.

A hidden gem nestled inside of The Monocle on Hertel Avenue, where nature‚Äôs magic meets the **warmth of community.**

Step into our ethereal escape, a queer-owned haven inspired by the beauty of the forest and the **spirit of belonging.**Savor the artistry of locally sourced dishes, sip on handcrafted cocktails, and lose yourself in the **cozy, inclusive vibe.**

Whether you‚Äôre here for brunch, coffee, or a whimsical cocktail adventure, stepping into the forest is always a good idea‚Ä¶

## Discover the magic within‚Ä¶

Step into a piece of history at Bewilderment, tucked inside a beautifully renovated Jewish synagogue alongside a boutique furniture store, a barber studio, an apothecary, and a design studio. Discover a hidden oasis where curiosity and creativity come alive in the heart of Buffalo. ūüĆŅ

![Image 1: the bar at cafe bewilderment](https://cafebewilderment.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/cafe_3_optimized.jpg)

##### Quality Without Compromise

We use sustainable, locally sourced ingredients to craft every dish and drink. From high-end coffee roasted by Stumptown to pastries and elevated small plates, everything is carefully curated for flavor and freshness.

![Image 2: coffee, cup and saucer, black coffee](https://cafebewilderment.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/g74d4bbf79852b066a4e491d56cfd8a61377425c7897fddc505a4df6fd6513443fec075c730d0f4f6d928745d57ed2fcc0f49895120281816d8f5711683e82fcc_1280-1572740-1024x682.png)

![Image 3: the seating area inside cafe bewilderment](https://cafebewilderment.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/cafe_4_optimized.jpg)

##### Inclusive, Safe & Cozy

As a queer-owned cafe, we aim to create a safe, welcoming space for all. We‚Äôre here to foster connection and provide a sense of belonging for everyone who walks through our doors.

![Image 4: Cafe 6 Optimized](https://cafebewilderment.com/wp-content/uploads/2024/12/cafe_6_optimized.jpg)

##### Ethereal & Natural

Inspired by nature and curiosity, our design and atmosphere evoke a cozy, woodsy vibe. Whether you‚Äôre stopping by for a morning coffee, a midday treat, or an afternoon cocktail, you‚Äôll leave feeling inspired and refreshed.
